name: Cwn Annwn
description: The Cwn Annwn (Hounds of Annwn) are spectral hounds from Welsh mythology, associated with the Wild Hunt and the Otherworld, Annwn. They are often depicted as being pure white or having fiery red eyes and dark red or black fur. These hounds are omens of death, their baying heralding an impending doom or a soul's passage to the afterlife. Led by figures like Gwyn ap Nudd, a ruler of the Otherworld, or sometimes by mythical figures like Arawn, they hunt lost souls or criminals. Hearing their baying is a sign of imminent death, and they are said to hunt on specific nights of the year, such as Samhain and Christmas. Unlike regular hunting dogs, the Cwn Annwn are not interested in earthly prey; their quarry is the souls of the dead. They are creatures of immense power and dread, embodying the connection between the living world and the realm of the dead. Their appearance is often linked to storms and wild weather, further emphasizing their connection to the untamed forces of nature and the Otherworld.
subtitle: Hounds of the Welsh Otherworld
appearance: The Cwn Annwn are typically described as large, spectral hounds with pure white or dark red/black fur and glowing, fiery red eyes. Some accounts mention that they have ragged, unkempt fur and a terrifying presence. They often possess an otherworldly glow and can appear and disappear at will. Their baying is said to be terrifying and instantly recognizable as a sign of impending death.
habitat: The Cwn Annwn are associated with the Otherworld, Annwn, and are said to roam the Welsh countryside, particularly during storms and on specific nights of the year, such as Samhain and Christmas. They are also found in desolate places like mountains, forests, and near ancient burial sites.
behavior: The Cwn Annwn are primarily hunters of souls, led by figures from the Otherworld. They are relentless and terrifying in their pursuit, and their baying instills fear and dread in those who hear it. They are not interested in earthly prey, focusing solely on their spectral quarry. Their appearance is often linked to times of transition and death, serving as an omen of what is to come.

keyMyths.title: The Wild Hunt
keyMyths.description: The Cwn Annwn are integral to the Wild Hunt, a spectral chase led by a figure like Gwyn ap Nudd. They pursue lost souls or those who have transgressed, representing the forces of the Otherworld encroaching on the mortal realm.

keyMyths.title: Omens of Death
keyMyths.description: Hearing the baying of the Cwn Annwn is a dire omen, signifying imminent death or a soul's passage to the Otherworld. Their presence is a stark reminder of mortality and the unseen forces that govern life and death.

sources.author: W. Jenkyn Thomas
sources.description: A collection of Welsh fairy tales and folklore, including stories of the Cwn Annwn.
sources.text: A collection of Welsh fairy tales and folklore, including stories of the Cwn Annwn.
